reviews.review_msg: A fun class. Dr. Elliott is knowledgeable in what she teaches. The best part of this course are the tutorials: You get to do fun activities. Journals take effort but they are worth it. To do well you have to really read all the materials and view the DVDs. Guest speakers are inspirational and takes the class to a higher level. Great class and prof!

AWESOME!  This class is amazing.  Extremely recommended.  It gives a new perspective on CJS in a way you can actually learn and reflect on.  It is not a class where you have to read and memorize but learning through interaction.  And if your shy, dont be!  Melissa (TA) is extremely approachable and creates a welcoming environment :)

Best prof I ever had, class is pretty easy, you just need to show up and write journals. There is not text book, you get to watch DVD's. Liz is really helpful, she has some good stories and keep the topics covered interesting. Good times :)
Avoid this class if you are not good in writting papers.

Don't take it by dist ed.  If you don't know how to write journals, you will have a problem.  There are no guidelines to begin with and the TA doesn't help much in that area.  They expect you to read 3 x 20pg articles and 3 ch. per week and integrate them into 3 pages while incorporating personal experience...most useless class I ever took.

I took this class by distance ed so I didn't get to meet the prof..but overall it was quite easy. The journal expectations were not clearly illustrated but it was ok. It was a bit time consuming thinking about your own, personal experience with restorative justice.
I took distance ed. so i dont know about the prof, but this course is by far the easiest upper crim course i took.
BRING EVEN MORE FENT!!!
I learned more about restorative justice from Saul Goodman

unorganized does not begin to describe her, does not post/open materials on time, provides criteria for papers then reveals weeks after the due date that it was graded on criteria never told to students to make it more difficult to get an A, the semester is almost done and we have not received a single grade back for the entire course

Brenda's lectures are pretty much pointless. Assignment guidelines/rubric are nonexistent. She is also extremely disorganized. The tutorials are just games and ice breaker activities. The TAs are nice but Brenda does not seem to put any effort into this class. We had a critical essay assignment due during reading week so that was quite annoying.
I cannot urge you enough donot take this course. The assignment guidelines and vague, Tutorials are a waste of time and the work load is huge and sporadic simply because this prof is unorganized.

Brenda is genuinely one of the worst profs I have had as a 4th year. She is so messy, we are a month into the course and she has been so unprepared. She doesn't reply to e-mails AT ALL, her lectures are so jumbled that I don't even watch them anymore because they teach nothing. Only plus is the TA so far is absolutely lovely

this class is so interesting so its extremely disappointing that for an online class students have to beg the professor to post the weekly materials and she neglects to open assignments til right before the deadline but does not provide extensions. She seems nice but sfu should have someone else who can actually support the students run the course

CRIM 315 as a 4-credit course was awesome. It was weekly worksheets, discussions, and tutorials. The tutorials were the only ones I liked my whole academic career! For anyone who didn't enjoy it, simply were too lazy to do the super easy weekly things. The papers were long which is the downside. She records her lectures, no attendance is needed.

Great and engaging tutorials. We would literally play games for about an hour. 2 papers, first one is 12-14 pages and the second one is 16-18 pages. I have no problem with Brenda because her lectures were online, to which I never attended. However, all you need to do is show up to fun tutorials and write papers.

Brenda was consistently having "wifi problems" which led to her posting lectures weeks late, or not at all.  She has high expectations for students engagement, but engaged very little herself.  Never answered any emails.  Appeared to be prioritizing other projects and pursuits over her students and the course.  Course papers were especially long.

(it was Crim 103)super sad excuse for a criminology class, worst class ive taken in my young academic career, she has so many different excuses to not show up for online lecture, the worksheets seemed to barely relate to course content. Dropped stats 203 to take this course, shouldve just stayed in there.

Best class of my undergrad. people who didn't do well did not work hard, because the TA and prof both had very clear instructions had 4th year students taken the initiative and simply asked. Brenda was so accommodating of my personal issues. Dana, Best TA ever. Don't hesitate to take this class, and put in the work to get the grade I got.

Brenda is a really nice lady, but she assigned so much homework and her papers are unnecessarily long. 
Would not take another class with her

The TAs will mark your papers so hard. This class is NOT a gpa booster. Final paper was 16-18 pages what can you even talk about past the 10th page?? Literally repeated myself through the entire essay.

She was pretty absent for the majority of the semester, as a person she is caring towards the students, however with her lack of presence due to attending conferences it was hard to evaluate her teaching style.

Krystal's class was unlike any other I've taken at SFU so far. Each class consists of a circle, mirroring restorative justice practices. The class requires a lot of participation and is not heavily focused on lectures. She brought in many guest lecturers, which really helped us to understand the theoretical material we were learning.

I took the course in the spring 2014. Dorota was so nice and helpful. Yes, you feel she is listening to you. I emailed her about my concern, she gave me advice and encouragement. Although it is an online course and I never have met her, I can feel her sincerity. She is a sweet person.
She is very helpful and nice. She is passionate about what she teaches.
One of the best TA's I've had! She's really friendly, and a fair marker. I would highly recommend her!

I thought this TA was pretty good.  She will go out of her way to help you, and seems to want to see people in the class do well.  This is not a good course to take by distance ed, but she does her best to help you learn and get through it.

Terrible TA who marks unfair and is very unhelpful. avoid at all costs. she didn't like me, so she gave me one percent lower than i needed for an A-. not amused. crim 315 is known to be a gpa boost class, but not in this case.

This prof is the most disrespectful, unhelpful, and ignorant person I have encountered in college, met with her 4 times to discuss a group project and asked for more guidance, she was so rude and made me feel so dumb. She threatened to take my group to the dean... She is so disrespectful and does not know how to teach. Avoid her at all costs.
she would give assignments which would be due the next day

Asad teaches this course with his passion, knowledge and experience. I've learned so much about RJ. So far, CRIM 315 is the best CRIM course I had at SFU! Definitely recommend anyone to take class with him. You will not regret!

Asad is very encouraging, kind and approachable. He is sometimes unclear with what he expects for assignments but after asking him questions he's very clear. Definitely recommend taking class with him if you ever get the opportunity to do so!
Asad is very approachable. Very helpful if you have questions. Different style teaching than most. He encourages conversation and discussion in lecture and tutorial

this guy is amazing and really cares about his students. his passion for RJ is incredible, which makes lecture engaging, and tutorials are a lot of fun! highly highly recommend anyone to take a class with him. you won't regret it.

Asad was my TA for CRIM 315. He is one of the nicest  people I have ever met. He made sure everyone was comfortable with any exercises we would do in our tutorial. He's also great at explaining to you what he expects from your paper. If you follow his basic guidelines, you will get a good grade for sure!

He is an absolutely amazing person and an even better teacher! Very patient, kind and understanding but expects the work to be done. He is incredibly helpful so really if you don't get a good grade it's because you didn't put the effort in! 100% recommend !!
I had great learning experience from his class. Strongly recommend students to take classes with Asad.
Great class! Really enjoyed tutorials and discussions.
I would recommend anybody to take class with him. Knowledgeable and passionate about restorative justice, provides details feedback and very helpful.

Tania as a person was incredibly sweet. However, as an educator I found them very hard to get along with and understand. Instructions and lectures were unclear. Deadlines were missed. Criteria for assignments were nearly impossible to understand. I found this class very sloppy and not structured. Again great person! Their learning style isnt for me

Interesting lecturer - rambles a bit, but nothing unmanageable and lectures only apply a bit to the two papers you need to write anyway. Get ready to read a lot to truly understand restorative justice and for the papers. Papers allow you to pick your own focus. Tutorials and TAs were awesome! Tutorials are basically two hours of fun games.

One of the most caring profs at SFU. Very approachable and nice. T.A's were just as nice! I enjoyed learning about the RJ & doing worksheets on it weekly. Papers were super fun to write and tutorials were filled with new activities. There's no exams, which made it easier to learn. i would for sure take again!!!!

very passionate about RJ and showcases that through lectures, tutorials, and assignments. You don't have to show up to lecture, because there's no exams. There are 2 papers, worth 60%. There are weekly worksheets, but they are so much to do and basically free marks. Tutorials can be dragging, since it's 2 hours and filled with activities, but easy

Lectures and course materials hardly related to the two papers that make up 60% of the final grade. Tutorials are just two hours of icebreaker games with no reference to course material that can be used in assignments. Criteria for assignments are extremely vague and if they just don't like what you write, you'll lose marks.
Literally amazing teacher! Having her for 315, and she is so passionate about the topic its incredible. You actually want to be in her class.

Amazing TA. Really cares about her students and really wants to challenge them emotionally in this course. If you are scared to speak, this tutorial/class is NOT  for you because you are really pushed to your limits to share experiences in front of everyone. Tania makes it so comfortable though as if she is your friend - not your TA!

Tania was extremely unclear in her paper expectations, which was beyond frustrating. Her lectures were pointless to go to in that her slides merely covered the readings, without adding much else. That being said, way too much info on slides. Often would talk in circles, hence the lack of clarity. As a person, she is great. As a prof, not the best.

Tania failed to provide clear instructions. All Powerpoint were summarization from readings. Didn't add additional insight. Although Tania is an expert in RJ, she really dried it out for me, as RJ is something I am truly passionate about.
